name: Health Care Professionals
description_short: Health Care Professionals Federal Credit Union is a member-owned financial cooperative established in 1975, serving over 3,400 healthcare workers in Richmond, Indiana with competitive rates and personalized service.
description_long:
Health Care Professionals Federal Credit Union was established in 1975 and is headquartered in Richmond, Indiana. As a federally chartered credit union, it operates as a member-owned financial cooperative specifically designed to serve employees of healthcare organizations. The institution has grown to steward over $35.8 million in assets and maintains a dedicated team of over 11 employees.

The credit union offers a comprehensive suite of financial products tailored to healthcare professionals' needs. Members can access checking and savings accounts (including regular savings with 4,700+ accounts and share draft accounts with 2,800+ accounts), certificates of deposit (85+ active accounts), credit cards, auto loans for new and used vehicles, personal loans, mortgages (both fixed-rate options under and over 15 years), wire transfers, and IRAs/KEOGH accounts. Digital banking services include online banking, mobile app access for iOS and Android, bill pay, remote deposit capture, e-statements, electronic signature authentication, and mobile payments. The credit union also provides financial counseling, educational workshops, and online calculators to help members manage their finances.

What distinguishes Health Care Professionals FCU is its highly specialized membership base restricted to healthcare workers, allowing them to tailor products and rates specifically for this professional sector. The institution maintains a small but personal operation with just one branch and two ATMs in Richmond, Indiana, enabling direct relationships with its 3,400-member base. The credit union emphasizes lower fees and competitive rates as a key value proposition, and offers drive-thru teller service for member convenience.

As a small, single-branch credit union, Health Care Professionals FCU is best suited for healthcare professionals in the Richmond, Indiana area who value personalized service and community-focused banking. The limited physical footprint and ATM network mean members outside the immediate area would face accessibility challenges. The organization serves its membership well but lacks the technological sophistication or product depth of larger financial institutions.
services: Checking accounts (Share Draft accounts with 2,800+ active accounts)
Savings accounts (Regular savings with 4,700+ active accounts)
Mortgages (fixed-rate 15 years or less, fixed-rate over 15 years)
Online banking and mobile app (iOS and Android)
Bill pay and online loan payments
Financial counseling and financial education workshops
pros: Specializes exclusively in healthcare professional membership, allowing tailored financial products and rates for this sector
Maintains competitive rates and lower fees compared to traditional banks, as highlighted in their marketing
Offers full-service banking including mortgages, auto loans, credit cards, and investment accounts like IRAs and CDs
Provides comprehensive digital banking including mobile app, remote deposit capture, bill pay, and online loan payments
Member-owned cooperative structure means profits benefit members rather than external shareholders
Established 1975 track record with $35.8 million in assets under management
Personal service with dedicated staff familiar with healthcare industry financial needs
cons: Single branch location in Richmond, Indiana with only 2 ATMs severely limits accessibility for members outside this region
Small membership base of 3,400 members may result in fewer product innovations and competitive features compared to larger credit unions
No public reviews available on the profile, limiting transparency about member satisfaction and actual service quality
Restricted membership eligibility limited to healthcare organization employees excludes many potential members
Limited digital infrastructure indicators—no mention of advanced features like investment services, wealth management, or comprehensive online tools
best_for: Healthcare professionals employed by member organizations located in or near Richmond, Indiana seeking personalized service
Members prioritizing lower fees and competitive rates over extensive branch networks or cutting-edge digital banking
Healthcare workers interested in credit union membership benefits and community-focused financial services
diagnosis: Health Care Professionals Federal Credit Union is best for healthcare professionals employed by member organizations in the Richmond, Indiana area who value personalized service, lower fees, and competitive rates. The main caveat is that its single-branch footprint and small membership base make it impractical for members outside the immediate region, and it offers a more traditional credit union experience rather than cutting-edge digital banking comparable to larger institutions.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
